tweets.name: SolanaFloor
tweets.content:
🚨JUST IN: @SECGov and @CFTC issue joint interpretive guidance clarifying how federal securities laws apply to crypto, introducing a formal token taxonomy across five categories: digital commodities, digital collectibles, digital tools, stablecoins and digital securities.

The agencies state that most crypto assets are not themselves securities, and clarify that activities like staking, mining, airdrops, and token wrapping are not inherently securities transactions.

Under digital commodities, the SEC includes assets such as Bitcoin, Ether, Solana, and memecoins like Dogecoin and Shiba Inu as examples.

The guidance also makes clear that tokenized stocks, bonds and other digital securities remain fully subject to securities laws, and that non security crypto assets may only fall under securities regulation when sold as part of an investment contract tied to profit expectations from issuer efforts.

tweets.name: SolanaFloor
tweets.content: 🚨NEWS: West Coast Stock Transfer announces adoption of @alpha_ledger’s Vulcan Forge platform to tokenize regulated securities on @Solana, starting with Nasdaq-listed Silo Pharma.

tweets.name: toly 🇺🇸
tweets.content:
THEY DID IT.

The SEC and CFTC just dropped a landmark document that officially classifies crypto assets.

They're actually telling us which crypto assets are securities and which ones aren't - by name!

THIS IS SOMETHING GENSLER REFUSED TO DO

(he focused on prosecuting crypto out of existence)

This rule doc gives crypto many of the benefits of the clarity bill - it lifts us out of the gray market - it gives every asset a path.

It's almost like the Clarity act just passed by way of regulator.

(of course, the actual clarity act will harden all this into legislation and make it irreversible in the event we get another Gensler, we still want it)

This rule says there's 5 categories for crypto assets:

1) Digital Commodities - assets tied to a functional, decentralized crypto system (e.g., BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP, ADA, DOGE). Not securities. (yes, they name them on page 14)

2) Digital Collectibles - NFTs, meme coins, artwork tokens, in-game items. Not securities (fractionalized collectibles may be an exception).

3) Digital Tools - membership tokens, credentials, domain names (e.g., ENS). Not securities.

4) Stablecoins - payment stablecoins under the GENIUS Act are not securities. Other stablecoins, it depends.

5) Digital Securities - tokenized versions of traditional securities. Like tokenized stocks. Always securities.

Amazing! This makes so much sense I can't believe it's coming from a regulator. 

No more enforcement threats to Ethereum developers and crypto exchanges.

How about the Howey test?

More common sense! If an issuer makes specific promises of managerial efforts from which buyers expect profits, the offering is a security until those promises are fulfilled. Then it's a commodity. The asset itself was never the security, the deal around it was. (E.g. XRP was a security pre launch, became a commodity after).

How about stuff like staking and mining?

Mining? Not a securities transaction.

Staking? Also not a securities transaction, that includes custodial and liquid staking even with LSTs!

How about wrapping BTC? Not a securities transaction.

Airdrops? NOT SECURITIES. NO MORE GEO BANS PROTECTING AMERICANS from free airdrops.

Remember this is a joint doc from the SEC and CFTC, They're actually cooperating on this, no internal strife, this is binding to both.

SEC regulates $80-100 trillion assets
CFTC regulates $5-10 trillion assets

Both of the world's largest capital markets are showing us that crypto assets are here to stay and they're welcome alongside traditional assets.

Every country will follow.

This is the biggest move toward legitimacy I've seen in all my time in crypto. Maybe bigger than the genius act since is covers all crypto assets.

Well done @MichaelSelig and @SECPaulSAtkins.

And especially well done to the indefatigable @HesterPeirce. Her fingerprints are all over this, couldn't have happened without her eight years of principles-based curiosity.

tweets.name: toly 🇺🇸

SOL is not a security.

tweets.name: Ben Zhou
tweets.content:
Bybit continues to strengthen its commitment to building a compliant and collaborative digital asset ecosystem in Georgia.

Following a meeting between CEO Ben Zhou and Prime Minister Irakli Kobakhidze, discussions focused on regulatory cooperation, fintech innovation, and the role of responsible growth in shaping the country’s digital economy.

“Georgia is becoming a model for how crypto companies can embed themselves into a country's financial fabric. The real measure of success is whether we’re making financial services more accessible, more affordable, and more connected for everyday users,” said Ben Zhou.

With a VASP license, PSP license, localized platform, and the launch of the Bybit Card, now the largest crypto card offering in the country, Bybit continues to expand its role across both trading and payments infrastructure.

Read more: https://t.co/YLKZwxs1lU

#Bybit #CryptoArk

tweets.name: 雨中狂睡SleepintheRain🦞
tweets.content:
USD1 活动马上就要结束了。从之前的节奏来看，过几天官方推出全新理财活动的可能性非常高。如果这几天窗口期 USD1 有一个明显的负溢价，我会去抄一些。

说下我的思路：
1、 $WLFI 质押提案通过，为其代币创造了新的需求——大户可以通过质押 $WLFI 进行 USDC、USDT、USD1 的1:1兑换，也就是允许质押代币的大户进行套利。

2、创造代币需求的核心目的并非拉升代币价格出货，而是希望通过市场买盘来维持代币价格，以方便团队持续地利用 $WLFI 来激励稳定币市值增长。这个判断是基于之前理财活动和 USD1 市值增长做出的判断。

既然明确 USD1 市值增长是团队的核心需求，那后续的理财活动就不会太少。低价买些去玩没什么太大的问题。

tweets.name: 雨中狂睡SleepintheRain🦞
tweets.content:
经过几天的研究，最高气温预测这个事儿总算是能做了。但要做到高盈亏比还要很长的一段时间去走。

大概思路是这样：

1、时间节点
确定自己的下注时间节点，哪个时间节点的胜率最高。我现在看下来，10:30之前就要决定仓位；

2、数据分析
我一般是和 AI 对话，自己先给到提示词和数据，让 AI 去给你完善你的提示词和数据源。评估数据对于当天天气变化的影响权重也非常重要；

3、变数
我会和 AI 说：在给出最终结论后，请列出两个可能推翻该结论的潜在因素或反向证据。要了解未来影响气温的数据变数，关注到并修剪仓位。如果变数没有出现，但赔率变动，那就是一个很好的加仓点位；

本身气温预测容量不大，所以也下不了太大的仓位，但胜在结算快，自然科学遵循一定的规则，黑天鹅事件少，也算是一个稳定赚猪脚饭的板块了。目前我还是一个气温预测小学生，仍在不断学习当中。

tweets.name: 憨厚的麦总
tweets.content:
之前聊过这个话题，熊市不要盯着突破做交易，或者觉得某个区间破了行情就来了，小区间的突破放大一个区间你会发现一百个阻力位。

牛市多Bear Trap，熊市多Bull Trap，但趋势是你的朋友，盯着lower high和lower low你就不会对趋势错判， 要做右侧就等趋势变了再去做，在此之前任何突破都当烟雾弹。
